title: Histological Observation of Tissues
author: Honghao Zhao
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.smfec3n

steps.title: Both white muscle and gonad tissues...
steps.instruction: Both white muscle and gonad tissues were taken from 250 tails per experimental group (AF/GF).

steps.title: White muscle samples were taken from...
steps.instruction: White muscle samples were taken from each fish in GF and AF experimental groups, and temporarily stored in tissue fixative..

steps.title: 500 gonad samples were also collected...
steps.instruction: 500 gonad samples were also collected from every experimental fish and kept in Bouin's fixative in 2 ml plastic tubes at 4 ℃.

steps.title: The back and abdominal muscle samples...
steps.instruction: The back and abdominal muscle samples were immediately harvested and frozen in liquid nitrogen, then were transferred and preserved at -80 ℃ until Oil red O staining.

steps.title: Serial transverse 10 μm-thick sections of...
steps.instruction: Serial transverse 10 μm-thick sections of muscles and gonads were stained routinely with hematoxylin & eosin (H&E). To determine the presence of fat in the muscles, frozen muscle tissues were stained with Oil red O solution, which would color any fat contained in the muscle.

steps.title: The sex of each grass carp...
steps.instruction: The sex of each grass carp was determined by the contour of the gonad and further results of the gonad tissue slice (Jensen and Shelton, 1983; Ke, 1975).

steps.title: The sex of the grass carp...
steps.instruction: The sex of the grass carp was determined by the contour of the gonad and further results of the gonad tissue slice. Based on the results of the sex determination, metabolomic analysis of muscle samples were divided into four test groups (n = 10): female fish of the grass feeding group (FGF), male fish of the grass feeding group (MGF), female fish of the artificial diet group (FAF), as well as male fish of the artificial diet group (MAF).

steps.title: A total of 200 - 400...
steps.instruction: A total of 200 - 400 fibers of per experimental group fish were studied using a Leica MZ 6 microscope for their cross sectional area. A size limit for identifying fibers was set at fiber diameters ≥ 10 μm as the optical resolution below this limit did not allow for sufficient identification and accuracy in the analyses.
